name: medication-timing-strip
title: Medication Timing Strip
description: Single-day 24h strip of one medication's dose markers with a live now cursor.

props.name: doses
props.description: { time, status, label? }[]; time is "HH:MM", "h:mm AM/PM", or a Date.

clinicalNotes: Times render in the patient's local timezone — a dose strip in the wrong timezone is worse than none, so parse and display consistently. Missed and skipped are distinct: missed is a gap, skipped is a deliberate decision (nausea, held dose), and the strip shows both for information only. It offers no catch-up guidance — whether to take a late dose, double up, or skip is drug-specific (anticoagulants, insulin, and levothyroxine all differ) and belongs to the caller or the label. The now cursor is client-side and hydration-safe. Statuses are shown by shape and legend text, not color alone, and the whole day is exposed to screen readers as text. This is the glanceable one-med sibling of a full schedule timeline.
notes: medcn-original, client (live now cursor, mount-guarded). Built from primitives + tooltip.
